txt: 'Higher realms'? Of course -- it all makes sense...
'It's cold now,' he was supposed to say. 'But someone has been maintaining it. The wiring has been repaired'...
'Paint the wall with your brains' dark.
*And* the B-side of the tape contains the instrumental version. It's like the world itself is telling you to do it. Only one obstacle stands on your way...
*BZZZZT*. Do you copy? This is RVD HQ. Anyone there?
*Did* you?
*It*.
*Kisses... kisses... kisses...*
*Off we go...* you see the hanged man's mouth open.
*Still* you can't shake the feeling that somehow these may bell do actually matter. If only a little bit.
*This economy* is a mysterious force, like cosmic weather. Mysterious and harsh.
... is what you want to say. You can barely get to 'you're under...' when you suddenly get weary...
... is what you're thinking. But you don't want to disco like this. Not really. Not in the deepest part of your soul, where the freckles only make you sad.
... is what you're thinking. But you don't want to party down. Not really. Not in the deepest part of your soul, where the freckles only make you sad.
... must have been... para-natural static. Ghosts... in the wires... Kill the connection... *BZZZZT*.
....as though you're supposed to be sharing some tremendous, evangelical secret...
...and is swept away, out of existence.
...and it hasn't even really started yet.
...is what you *want* to say. But it isn't that easy, is it?
...is what you almost say, but the words choke in your throat. Do you really want to miss out on all this good stuff? Perhaps it's *significant*.
...is what you're about to say when a cold streak runs through your heart. Once said, there's no going back, Harry.
...the shed will be gone, and you won't even remember that the tree was ever there, much less what kind of tree it was, a pine or a birch. You won't remember if a man had been hanging from it.
11:11. It's *meaningful*.
A bad omen. Beware of this church.
A bloodstained killer.
A chance to establish contact with the future! What a beautiful, *terrible* thought...
A clue about the person behind all the murders.
A completely different world.
A dark feeling. This is going wrong. Let go of the clutch.
A diagram for summoning some time-forgotten being? The symbols seem very esoteric.
A dream?
A feeling of tenderness washes over you -- a longing even, perhaps. And gentle tragedy.
A glorious sight: your body, tired from the vastness of the visions that haunt it,  burning up in a flame fed by your own living breath.
A golden pendant hangs around the woman's neck, in the shape of what looks like a tiny fish head trapped in amber.
A laughing skull. Death hilarious. This is gonna be baaaad...
A little *me*-time in the abyssopelagic zone... you can never return to it now. Only detective-work remains. Wakefulness and detection.
A little rhyme jingling in your mind's attic... "Of the future, of the past/ Of all that is not made to last"...
A lot can happen in four months. Especially in winter. The winters are never easy on you, of that you are sure.
A massive pit opens up in your stomach and the most terrible feeling comes over you.
A new dimension of reality will open. A portal is this bed of waste. Sleep there and awaken *elsewhere.*
A pity. The mystery of *you* will have to remain a mystery for the time being.
A prayer of sorts? To Revachol.
A strange feeling... Every now and then, something feels off about the way she speaks. She doesn't change tone, but you feel as though there's more about her than she lets on.

A strange feeling... like before, when you caught her lying better than she should have. Every now and then something feels off about the way she speaks. She doesn't exactly change tone, but you feel as though there's more about her than she lets on.
A strange feeling... looking at the water. Maybe you should just wander off into the sea? Leave it all and walk in...
A strange sensation of loss. When she left the Earth, the dust and the ice and the humans... That is unimportant to the quiz. Stop thinking about this.
A strange word... *treatment*.
A trash container. Yellow and capsule-like, lying in the corner of the yard.
A tremendous loneliness comes over you. Everybody in the world is doing something without you.
A trusted friend left behind...
A-a-afraid? What is she afraid of?
ASTRAL PROJECTION! Be *open-minded* about this.
Abort! You clearly have not thought this through. You won't like what you will see there -- and you will never *un-become* it.
Absolutely *in* the question! First we find a *sad* banger, then we sing this place to SHIT.
According to Klaasje, Lely said his real name wasn't really *his* -- perhaps that's because he was fostered?
Acknowledged. Connection established. *BZZZZT*. Welcome aboard, Remote Viewer. You are a special man, and *definitely* not imagining it.
Actually, it's called *extraphysics* and you don't need to be a magus to access it.
After all this, you still haven't found the answer to the one question that matters: Who *is* Dick Mullen?
After life, death. After death, life again. After the isola, the pale. After the pale, isola again.
Alcohol and heartbreak.

All of this, accompanied by a musical composition telling of the heroism of old, when men were bigger in both body and heart. You feel strange kinship to this old soldier. You and him are the same now.
All those empty wine bottles and cigarette butts on the ground... Someone *partied* really, really hard here.
All you will find here is pain.
Also -- what if the bad thing happens again?
Also of note -- your unconscious racial profiling machine ruled out Messinian before. Despite all the *copporoonies* and *cobos*. That leaves Vespertine or Oranjese. Continue.
An eerie feeling rises in your chest...
An empire without an emperor.
An ominous warning of bad things ahead.
An ominous, foreboding feeling fills you, as you look at the tie.
And a hole in his heart. That first.
And dangerous. Dangerous too.
And more than that: you're sad. There's an absence inside of you, something missing that may have been freckled too.
And so all our lives become but a faded memory, an ephemeral vision of a Van Eyck concert flyer... blink or you'll miss it.
And then there is you. And what happened to *your* mind. Are you not a saeraff in worship too?
And your chest swells with pride -- look! It's made right here in Revachol, by Saint-Batiste, a proud Revacholian pharmaceutical company with great *traditions*!
Another apocalyptic actor in town?
Another identity misplaced... There must be some cosmic conspiracy afoot!
Anything to not face the hanged disco man yet...
Apricot blossoms. White blossoms erupting. A sensation, like cold hands on your face.
Apricots.
Are you actually friends? Or just colleagues thrown together by circumstance?
Are you s-s-sure about that? Something about her gives you a *bad* feeling. What kind of *secret* is she hiding?
Are you s-s-sure you want to do that? Remember what happened *last* time...
Are you sure? It's probably too late to be a Tequila *Sunrise* now.
Arm yourself with anything you can. Unless you have... nothing? Because you didn't find your lost gun? Then just walk to your death.
As he says so you feel the young woman looking at you... and get a *feeling* you can't quite put your finger on. A suspicion?
As if it's self-explanatory, beyond patriotism. A fact.
As if said by someone else. Someone outside you. Around you. Not you.
As if under a microscope.
As she says so you feel the young woman looking at you... and get a *feeling* you can't quite put your finger on. A suspicion?
As the words leave your mouth -- there it is again! That strange feeling, like there's something *in* there...
As you do, you hear and the echo of the Doomed Commercial Area. Its black halls and dusty machines. Then the feeling passes.
As you look back you think: So love did *do him in* after all.
As you say the words you find yourself peering from the corner of your eye... to where? To the West!
As your gaze instinctively turns north, a small black pit opens up in your stomach.
As your hand reaches for the small rectangle of glossy paper, it snaps free from the branch...
At the back of your head you feel the rapid beat of anodic music... then it fades away.
Awful lonely here. No ruckus. No old men playing their little ball game.
B-b-but what if you don't *like* what you find there? There'll be no going back from it...
Be afraid. You smell wrong.
Be honest with him.
Be prepared. Make sure you have your pepperbox in your hand.
Be prepared. Wield Ruby's double-barrelled pistol.
Be wary of those mysterious ornaments from the *dark isles*.
Beauty has abandoned you in this ugliness, honcho. And then some! You cannot even remember the *name* of beauty; what beauty was...
Because it's lonely. Just like you. Seaweed and strange fish around it, limpets clinging to the carcass... The sounds of the streets above...
Because it's your last lifeline to being a police officer instead of a hobo.
Beckoning you to come to it.
Behind her the window has been boarded up. You sense the boards creaking, twisting for a second... and some kind of *doubt* in her tense shoulders.
Below the pathetics -- terror. Do not look into its blue heart.
Below... in the Union box... was there something behind the window? In the hawthorn branches, brushing against the glass.
Beside his orderly handwriting, the bullet looks especially sad. Like a tiny, shrivelled head of cauliflower.
Besides, if you never open it, you're never gonna find out, what's *behind* the door.
Better not touch it. Who knows what evils it may hide.
Beware. They're very *painful* secrets.
But does it have anything to do with necroplasmic life forms? 'Ghosts' in everyday parlance.
But even just holding the tape makes you feel a little sad...
But he *is*... you can feel it. Or maybe it's something else then!
But he's not here to say it. Something *else* got in the way. Events *intervened*...
But it is! It is!
But somehow it doesn't scare him, like it scared you. Doesn't make him quiver with cold. Poor guy...
But still, somehow you *knew* it was a communist.
But then it went bankrupt... your skin crawls from making the connection!
But they seemed so mysterious.
But this reeks of sadness!
But what if the information is vital -- on *the hunt*...
But whose?
But you don't like that, you liar. You like this one, the softness of her core is dear to you...
But you feel the dried flower in your hand somehow still *does* matter. Although not to this sad old man.
But you're not a regular animal. You're not even a regular cop. You see -- and hear! -- beyond the veil.
But you... you *feel* you should have it on you. Something bad might happen.
But... but... what if you never find out how *your* story ends?
But... didn't the Wild Pines representative say that Revachol would *resolve* history?
By other people.
Calls herself *the Pigs?* That sounds bad.
Can it be? We haven't heard that name in ages. Welcome to the RVD, Firewalker. *BZZZZZT*. You are a special man, and *definitely* not imagining it.
Caustic Echo. It was darkness all along. But you were young and it was summer. You just had to take a dive...
Check your pockets! Check your... holy fuck, you really don't know where it is, do you?!
Close your eyes. Reach out with your *feelings*. There's something *para-natural* at play here.
Cold drops of rain fall onto your outstretched hand. A slight tremble passes through it...
Compared to the intersection, in front of Video Revachol 24 -- it's nothing. Compared to that, everything is possible.
Connections to other worlds. Worlds past the Insulindian, unknown to you. You only know you've never been there.
Cool.
Could there have been a *right* way out of this garden of forking paths, you think?
Could this be the malicious Entity? Perhaps it's wise to go along with this *masquerade* for now...
Countless lines separate life and death. What terminal boundary was crossed here? Men like Titus don't murder over nothing. Ask him again.
Crisis, death. You can feel it in your blue soul.
Crying is good, it gives your performance authenticity. Besides, you already know the lyrics since you've listened to it, like, a million times!
DETECTIVE
DON'T COME BACK HERE!
Dark cloud? That sounds unpleasantly familiar...
Dark orange flames reflect in her green eyes. An oil fire on the ocean.
Delicate and fragile, they feel alien to the world around you. Out of place somehow.
Didn't... didn't that curtain just *move*?!
Dive for other clues now, like you always do. Make it up in the deep end...
Do *not* trust him. For all you know, it might be *his* name by which he is addressing you. You need confirmation.
Do it. Find her husband. This will *surely* lead to a cryptozoological mystery with that *extremely rare insect*...
Do it. Find him. This will *surely* lead to a mystery with that *extremely rare insect*...
Do it. Who knows what cryptozoological mysteries will be uncovered?
Do what you need to do. And then keep moving toward the future.
Do you *feel* that?
Do you know what takes place upon mist-shrouded Semenese islands? Don't listen to Encyclopedia -- they're inhabited by shamans and wildmen practising unnatural mysticism.
Do you realize what's out there? Upon the mist-shrouded islands of Ile du Fantôme, in Semenine? Eerie pygmies and wild shamans practising unnatural mysticism?
Do you think that's what happened to the man you found? Do you think he wanted *out*?
Do you think that's what happened to the man you found? Do you think he was trying to *just have fun*?
Does it? It feels like a *lot*. Or maybe even more than a lot. It feels like *too much*.
Does this *feel* like a *good* thing though?
Doesn't look too incriminating -- but still feels like a *find*.
Don't be fooled. The bad times have already begun.
Don't be too hard on yourself. You couldn't have known that for sure. And, besides, finding this phasmid is your special destiny -- you can feel it!
Don't let her become complacent! She still needs to ward *her soul* against the evil forces!
Don't listen to the lieutenant! He doesn't know *anything*.
Don't rip the red one out -- you'll awaken the ghosts!
Don't say it! Are you sure? This may be the only chance you get to touch it...
Don't turn around, don't turn around!
Don't worry. One day.
Don't! Maybe you shouldn't...
Dora. The name feels like a *gift*. A gift that was meant for you -- to make it possible to live.
Down a deep, black well.
Draped in ancient sadness. Are you sure you want to remember this bit of historic trivia? Standing under her long slender form like this, dwarfed...
Drawn by regret. Stop, before you *hurt* yourself with the Tutti Frutti wrapper. Throw it away, please.
Drinking coffee and smoking. With a friend.
Dust rises before you like mist. A tomb? Haunted by regal spirits from distant ages...
Even the darkness is holding it's breath inside the apartment.
Exactly. Back out of this corner of your mind by choosing the bottom-most option: "To hell with this."
Exactly. It's measured, level-headed, and it needs to be heard.
Exactly. So you know what to say.
Father Jairzinho? Sounds like some kind of mystic. Or a magician.
Fear and apprehension... You should ask what's out there first.
Feels as though you should do more honourable things. As if there's some kind of a... secret reward. If you just commit enough honourable acts.
Feels good, doesn't it? Doing the right thing.
Feels like you should reply with the very pinnacle of idiocy here, so that things get totally transcendent. But you haven't gotten there yet, so you don't know what to say.
Feels like you should've used vinegar too...
Feels like you've failed the biggest shit-test of them all...
Feels so, so familiar.
Feels very, very familiar. Harry. Please. You were supposed to discard it.
Finally -- a chance to connect with the *divine*.
Finally, something to calm the angry spirits that have been plaguing you.
Fine. It's not a phasmid. It's not even organic. Just a fire iron resting by the fire place. But... where's your sense of wonder, man?
Five days. Not more. Maybe sooner.
Flakes of snow fall onto your outstretched hand. A slight tremble passes through it...
Flashes of bloody and terrible scenes of war. Men dying, horses shrieking, blood sinking into sand and snow and asphalt and fertile black earth...
For all time.
For good or ill, the deed is done.
For some reason this *does* strike you as the most plausible theory of them all.
For some reason, the words don't come out. Instead you instinctively touch your collar to check your necktie.
Forget about that. What's with this Du Bois stuff? You're getting some seriously bad vibes from that name...
Forty-one, Precinct 41. A massive pit opens up in your stomach and the most terrible feeling comes over you.
Fragments on bone scattering, like your thoughts at this moment, sticking now to forebodings, now to a strangely familiar voice...
Frankly it's a bit terrifying.
Fresh blood. The return to her side.
From here it looks as though the clown-faced man is screaming. The tendons of his jaw are torn apart, hyoid ripped from the force of the lieutenant's hands.
Fuck love! This is your *GUN*, this is serious.
Garte. You have to convince Garte to let you sing karaoke in the Whirling. After you've won him over, you can *express* yourself. Let the pain out. Make everyone understand.
Ghosts, speak to me!
Go... to the trash container...
Good call! Never mind that *old trash container* lurching in the corner of this yard, full of  old news...
Good call! Never mind that *old trash container*lurching there, full of bad old news...
Good, these people know your *true* name. Looks like it has preceded you, Mr. Sunset. More on that later.
Good. Be ready to take damage.
Good. Now back out of this corner of your mind by choosing the bottom option: "To hell with this." You've been standing here long enough.
Great. This is great shit. You need more.
Hard to say, it's gone now. Feelings pass, you see. Especially the small ones.
Hard to see, but easy to feel. Somehow this *will* change things.
Have mercy on yourself. Take the door.
Have you gone over everyone? Is anybody missing?
Have you stirred the ghost of the Doomed Commercial Area from its rest? Could this be its dismembered heart, beginning to flutter?
He *seems* sure, but you are left with the nagging doubt you might have overestimated the hard coreness of that jam...
He avoided looking her straight in the eye -- you would've too.
He can't hurt you. What more can he do that you haven't gone through already?
He doesn't have a home. His home is under a boat.
He doesn't have a home. Not anymore.
He is not going to become an entrepreneur.
He is secretly admiring your sea of muscles. Everyone is.
He looks almost as old as you.
He means something para-natural. He must...
He seems to have his own take on the conflict played out in perpetuity by these toys. Might be interesting to find out what it is...
He sees in the dread moose something perversely beautiful and just.
He should still know. You have to be *forewarned* about these things.
He wants to keep it at a distance. He's afraid of what powers are contained therein...
He was *just* about to head home. The first step back home proved to be his last.
He was like a cleric, a shepherd.
He will never play pétanque again.
He's going to leave you alone again. That's sad.
He's not gonna get over it.
He's not gonna make it.
He's right. You could smell it, that something missing, even under the overpowering odour of the corpse.
He's so close to envisioning what's beyond. Just needs a little push over the edge...
Heartbreak Welkin.
Heartfelt gratitude -- but does it feel like closure? What *really* happened?
Help him surface the pain, release the pressure. Be a friend.
Her gloves -- you get the feeling that you need them. You have a dead body to deal with, after all.
Her?
Here comes the dark. The plug will be pulled... now.
Hey, but you're still in a ghost house... What if someone heard this? What if they know you're here?!
Hidden, distant, kept safe from you.
His corpse is marked by stars.
His flesh is cold. Icy.
His glasses turn golden as the fire reflects off the lenses. Once again, you are filled with the certainty: this man is your brother.
His glasses turn golden as the fire reflects off the lenses. You feel this man is your brother.
Hmm... it's as if there is an automatic *self-defence* structure in your hand, keeping you from mind-fucking yourself with letters from past lovers.
Hmm... you feel like you should come back to this *thing*-based questionnaire if you see anything interesting in the Whirling later.
Holy fuck! It's coming to get us!
Holy shit! A disembodied voice! Have cursed void-phantasms come to haunt us for misdeeds done in the past?
Home? To work? To get something from somewhere? Who knows what people are doing when we're not looking.
Hopelessly alone behind the unbreakable walls he spent a lifetime erecting. No one will ever know him.
Host almighty, he's a total bummer now!
How do you know it already hasn't?
I DON'T WANT TO SEE YOU. I DON'T WANT TO SEE YOU EVER AGAIN! STOP CALLING ME! GET LOST FROM MY LIFE, YOU RUINED IT!
I think it was established that *you* did it. Anyway.
I wonder how long has it been since you last hugged someone?
I wouldn't say it is.
I-i-is there anybody th-th-there?
If it weren't for her he would not have been there. The shot would not have connected with his soft palate...
If only you knew...
If that's true then *Revacholian lit* would fit you like a glove.
If the Whirling is part of the same building then it's part of the Doomed Commercial Area. The darkness of this place is there too...
If you can't trust your own eyes, who can you trust...
If you can't trust your own eyes, who can you trust? Certainly not Kim. He's so... suspicious.
Ignore the lieutenant. He's never connected with anything para-natural. You're on different planes, metaphysically speaking.
Images of your body smashed against the pavement flood your mind -- this is *dangerous*.
Imagine it. An explosion. Of *stars*. Good bye world of men, money and machines...
In a pyramid? Now that *would* be something...
In the West is the miracle...
In the back of your head, you feel the rapid beat of anodic music... then it fades away.
In the darkness, you sense her eyes on you, inspecting you with their multicoloured glass. As if you're a bug under a microscope.
In the northeast a dust mite stands on the north coast of Caillou. In a bookstore. It's you.
In the still mirror of the bay you see Martinaise, reflected. Tall edifices of ruins reach into the water, like shimmering towers. And the shacks too. Pine trees and motor lorries, upside down...
In there she is alone, trapped in a world of blue and red lights.
In this yard? Someone thought they could have a summer house on the beach. For cheap. It didn't work out. Looks like they abandoned it.
Interesting... you still have to find a copy though. Before you can blast it.
Interesting... you still have to find it, however.
Investigate the blood red chair? You're going to leave it alone, Harry. That's what you're going to do.
Is it? Can you be sure? What if you are secretly being manipulated by La Puta Madre as you pursue this investigation. What if he wiped your memory on purpose?
Is it? You don't feel like it is. You feel like you should make up for it by *scrutinizing* him extra carefully...
Is she pitying you? Good god, she's pitying you.
Is that an accusatory glance he gave you? No... no, he's cool.
Is that the phasmid? It looks like the phasmid, leaning against the wall...
Is that what it is -- this feeling?
Is, is that *true*? Have you felt *estranged* from your own self recently?
Islets and posts like stepping stones lead into the water in front of you. Go. Step in... it's been too long.
Isn't that odd? How *can* you run so much?
It certainly feels like whatever you are will die with you.
It could be. She was strange.
It did not come as a surprise to him. He might actually not be bullshitting.
It feels like there's an entire history behind the word... but it doesn't really matter. You got it. You know what it *means*.
It feels like you'll get to know -- soon enough.
It feels like you're peeking into someone's home residence. Inside it's private, cozy, warm. Dusty, too.
It is but one of the many strange *optic-atmospheric* phenomenon on this wondrous archipelago. You're sure you once saw sundogs -- in your youth. And blue flares....
It is not clear. All that is known is: Sleep in the trash and there is *no coming back.*
It is.
It killed himself to *spite* you.
It looks almost ethereal -- is it even real?
It may work, you think, but why do you feel like there may be a terrible cost?
It must be cold and lonely down there, in the icy water.
It must be very close... maybe, just maybe it will come toward you...

It takes time to block out the sounds of the world. But once you do, it's like you're on a wavelength. Not like any *natural* wavelength. No. There seems to be *something* on the other end, if only you could listen closely enough...
It was a retaliation for your earlier assault... the trees... are they plotting something?
It was dark in the shack. The waves outside had calmed down. She looked at the loaded gun, then she cracked the barrel open and took the bullet out. Not today.
It was just trying to help....
It wasn't time yet. You have to know of the girl... Return when you know more.
It would be better if you didn't have to. It's not just your own life that will be at stake.
It's *definitely* your style to solve *side-shit*.
It's *really* time to stop this now.
It's *way* more than that.
It's a clue!
It's all gone now. You never became a poet or an entroponaut.
It's an ode to self expression.
It's as if they're *real* wizards, able to resurrect dead real estate and breathe life into bank accounts.
It's because of all the money. Even the sky gets an aura of glistening lucre.
It's been through a whole *transformation*. There are so many insights to share -- it just needs a medium. Wouldn't the wake-walkers pay to hear the somniloquy of the spirit light?
It's better not to know, that's what the wind is telling you.
It's gone. Your gun is gone. There's nothing in your pockets.
It's gotta be the phasmid this time. Look -- it moved!
It's home.
It's just like that woman in the electronic doorbell... the circuit ghost...
It's like you see your own face reflected back at you in the vanishing geometry -- a strange, alien image that's supposed to be you.
It's lost forever. Into the deepest of seas.
It's meaningful, I *swear*! You just wait... I've got the case nearly busted wide open.
It's no good, Harry. Her voice rings in the evening air. It smells of burning buildings...
It's not a ghost story. It's a curse -- and Garte ought be made knowledgeable so he can perform counter-spells.
It's not easy to reach a harmonic resonance of sines without some adjustment.
It's not the locals he's worried about -- but an eleven year old girl.
It's not the phasmid... It's not even organic -- it's a fire iron. How disappointing!
It's not too late, no one's going to blame you for backing out. You don't have to do this. Just get out.
It's nothing. Yes, that's *probably* right. It's nothing. Forget about it, filter it out.
It's one of those ink-blot tests. An invitation to connect with the *divine*.
It's only a red chair. Just a red chair in an empty shack -- with what looked like a dusty bow tie on the shelf. Nothing to see here, right?
It's strangely comforting to think of these voices travelling through the air, until you capture them, firefly-like, with your little metal antenna...
It's the warmth of a winter night's fire. Maybe she could give you comfort and shelter? Some cigarettes and food money? Maybe she's your...
It's very unlikely. Everyone scattered once the tribunal started. They're probably fine.
Just a feeling. A warning from some part of you.
Just a moment. That voice isn't human, and it's not saying 'Nay!' -- It's saying 'N-n-n-neigh!'
Just felt right. Why, do you want to be a Tequila *Sunrise?* It's a little too late for that...
Just like how the crazy daredevil piloting that vehicle must have accelerated into the sea!
Just steer clear of this one, okay. There's something terrible about this one.
Keep an eye on these trees. They're up to no good.
Keep at it. There must be a reason you've looked out this window so many times.
Keep your ears peeled, then. If the species really has migrated to Martinaise, you're sure to hear it again.
Large flakes of snow fall onto your outstretched hand. A slight tremble passes through it...
Le Retour...
Less talk, more feeling. Keep your eyes closed, soak in the closeness. See what the feeling entails.
Let's call it an apparition from the past.
Life has left it, unlikely to ever return.
Life is garbage.
Like a Tequila Sunset.
Like at a beach party. With sand and sun and seagulls dancing on the breeze.
Like in a circus! When the circus leaves town and they tie a black spotted giraffe to the wall of a carry-pen!
Like the scales of some ancient white monster, cracked and pearly.
Loneliness.
Look at it. Of course it was you. You *partied* here.
Look at that lady. Take a *gander*. Squint your eyes, bub.
Look closer!
Looking straight at you. Helpless, trapped within itself.
Looks like this furnace has a face and it's a face of agony.
Lucky goats. The fog looks soft and inviting.
Man, it's lonely doing this alone. The wind seems to howl in a strange manner...
Marked by the devil itself!
Maybe it's the old you? Or maybe she was just paranoid...
Maybe there's some kind of reason why they're laughing? An interesting reason somehow tied to the case!
Maybe you can ask her for some *stock tips* -- that's a *maybe*. Or a *job*. But you're sure as hell too ashamed to ask for cash buddy.
Maybe you should check that cockatoo book again... What if the cockatoo is your *heraldic bird*?
Maybe you should... What if the cockatoo is your *astral captain*? Or your *heraldic bird*?
Meaning she's the voice of all those living souls!
More precious than the gun, the badge and the motor carriage *combined*. Lost forever. Into the deepest of seas.
Motor carriage, motor carriage... Something bad with a motor carriage.... A dark lump rises in your throat.
Move away from this scene and you don't have to think about it anymore. Just walk away and it's *gone*!
Mute silence as always.
N-n-n-no. This isn't right...
Nature was not capable of more.
Needless to say, you *must* ask her about the mysterious phasmid.
No need to beat yourself down over it. It probably wasn't the definitive piece of the puzzle. Still, somehow it feels like May bells matter somehow.
No one is saying the multi-patterned necktie you found tied to the ceiling fan can *talk*. No one. It must be merely *imagination*, but...
No one knows. It is a fleeting feeling.
No reply. How ever many times you keep asking.
No storm will ever drown Revachol, the great solution to the riddle of history.
No thoughts to come back to, no scenes to replay.. It feels calm the way deep sleep is supposed to be.
No! They're still there. You can *feel* them back there.
No, it was something else... It was *eerie*.
No, it's not that... It's the stale smell of rubber, the squeaky sound of sneakers, your bruised knee against the mat. And a whistle... then the feeling is gone.
No, it's the troubled colour of her energetic vibrations!
No, no, it can't be that. You're not Harry Du Bois. Forget you heard any of this. There's no time to deal with this now.
No, no, no -- she's got it all backwards. It's reality that's *obscuring* what's real. You have never been closer to the dark truths of existence...
No, no, no, no...
No, no. These are *terrible* colours. They're like... like...
No, no. You cannot get off that easy. This is serious. Lives are at stake.
No, something's wrong here... are you sure she's talking to *you*?
No, there's more to this. You get this strange *feeling*.
No, this is right. Who doesn't long to believe in *miracles*?
No, you *know* this tone of voice. You've heard it before. She's about to deliver some bad news...
No, you don't. And it's better that way.
No-no, don't sing the happy song, it's stupid. Sing the sad song, it's profound.
No-no, not the voice! Make it stop...
No-no-no! This was supposed to take your mind *off* the other thing!
No-no-no. There's *something* here, you can feel it!
No-no. You need the *wisdom*.
No. A strange feeling fills your gut and suddenly you *know* the gun is empty. Thinking about it makes you nauseous.
No. An ominous feeling fills your gut and suddenly you *know* the gun is empty. Thinking about it makes you nauseous.
No. Communism killed him, remember? Love made it possible. You know this much.
No. How about you keep sticking around? Look around -- find the company representative, see what they have to say? Maybe the company will help? Then get back to this.
No. It's not true. It was there. It was real. You remember every detail of its sounds, its movements, its presence...
No. No. It's just a fear. Even if... who cares. No one wants you anyway.
No. The miracle is still waiting. This is the darkness.
No. There is more here. A sliver of something. Some kind of future.
No. You *feel* like it was something else -- but what?
No.
No... Too late. It's gone... There is no ringing anymore. Just the sound of the streets.
Not like he thinks, but you can feel it. Somehow this *will* change things.
Not so! You sense their strange magicks, cast from the dark forests they writhe around in. Don't believe it? Go ahead, straight through the curtains. Suffer the consequences.
Nothing. It's nothing. Nothing more to see here.
Nothing. Its simple eyes give off no flicker of light, no one is home. The twitches of its limbs are insect-like, unknowable, perhaps without substance even.
Nothing. Just a hunch. The hunch passes, leaving you there, by the old buoy bobbing in the water. Time to go.
Nothing. Just slow disappearance. You look at a tree or a coal shed, then look away, and, turning back...
Nothing. Just threats that passed by -- outside in the world. Ill omens from another life. Not this one. You ignored them. Good. The sleep is nourishing. Take more of it.
Nothing. Nothing. It's probably nothing, forget I brought it up. Please proceed with the carefree lollygagging.
Nothing.

Now look at you, you misery-clad simian, barely able to tie your own laces. Your armpits are lakes, a scythe of booze precedes you. Your hair sticks to your forehead and your underwear feels uncomfortable...
Now that you say them, the words feel like tin, heavy, they're coming from the bottom of you. You've said them half a thousand times...
Now! Now you will *finally* get to know who you are!
Now, now detective... always the sceptic.
Now, the time has come for you to be inducted into the ranks of the Remote Viewers Division.
Numerous empty wine bottles glisten in the rain. Someone *partied* really, really hard here.
OH GOD! WATCH OUT!
OH MY GOD, WHY ARE YOU CALLING ME? STOP CALLING ME! ARE YOU DRUNK AGAIN?
Odd...
Of course he doesn't. He's DEAF.
Of course you are! It's too late to be a Tequila *Sunrise* now... there will be no sunrise, not for you.
Of course you could sing this! You could take sad to a whole new level with this. And you already know the lyrics since you've listened to it, like, a million times!
Of course you do, Harry. Everyone does.
Of course you do. You don't even know *what* it is you're feeling, but you can feel it. That's the mark of a true *Remote Viewer*...
Of course. At this point, precise measurements of your soul can only be performed from the outside. It needs to be heard.
Of course. Do it. Who knows what mysteries await?
Of course. Everything's okay.
Of course. Like a small animal that only comes out at night. All it needs -- is to be heard.
Oh *fuck* yes. Go there in your head. It's been days since you've thought about her. A week...
Oh god, tell us you haven't done it *again*. Are you twice the killer now?
Oh god, why didn't you think of this before -- COPS HAVE GUNS! Where's yours?!
Oh god, you're going to be in this church forever. FOREVER.
Oh no! The sound -- it's moving away, somewhere over there -- go after it!
Oh no. You've made *el bigo mistako*. He's going to go on forever!
Oh yeah. Here comes the *interesting*.
Oh yeah. Time to do the damage.
Oh, *requests a description*, huh? We'll *give* him one. Describe the Blaasma Gun.
Oh, but you are: too ashamed to ask this person for *money*, too scared to belittle yourself in her eyes -- those half-precious stones of Eau-de-Nil.
Oh, no. Tell us you didn't *really* kill him.
Okay then. Over-mystified that a bit..
Okay! Maybe it's something else then.
Okay, it's gone. Your gun is most definitely gone.
Okay... you're one of the initiated. Then go ahead and fiddle with the curtains. But do so at your own PERIL.
Olden days were golden days. Life's lustre is lost on those who speed.
On this mere cameo. I get it. Moving on.
Once you get in, that's it -- one pull of the starter handle and you're off into the bay. A strange trepidation comes over you: are you sure you want to go *now*?
One of the flyers has gotten stuck to the branches of a small brush. The sound of it flapping in the wind is an echo of Van Eyck.
One you should be *ashamed* of. It's no wonder the soft one doesn't want it back. It was right to abandon you...
Only *curiosity* could account for stepping over that threshold. Maybe there's treasure in there? A white alligator? A fountain of quicksilver?
Only a strange little sadness remains.
Only banal things strike you. At the core, you're a very banal person, with a very small soul.
Oops. A bad feeling comes over you...
Oops...

Or has it been consigned there as punishment? You feel as though this creature is your *friend* and wants to reattach itself to your neck. So that you may continue your adventures together in this strange world!
Or maybe you were trying to smash your own reflection in the windowpane?
Or was it communism that killed him and love that did him in...
Or what if you are...?
Peer into a discarded coffee cup!
Perfidy? Oh no, we're afraid the presence is VERY REAL.
Perhaps he's on his way to where you just came from -- into the primordial darkness?
Perhaps the mysterious music is somehow connected to the case? A rusálka, or a half-demonic *apsara*, singing...
Perhaps there's someone there? Under the water. Waiting for you.
Perhaps you should break into apartments more often?
Perhaps you suspected it *before* you took the case.
Perhaps you truly *are* the one to deliver this woman from the doom...
Plaisance needs to hear about this. Perhaps if you combine your *psychic energies* you'll make sense of the situation.
Plaisance was right... There's an entity living in the chimney! You should ask her about the curse.
Please, don't open that door.
Please. Relax.
Plus one. Ruby.
Precisely, Satellite-Officer Vicquemare. It's Martinaise.
Probably for the best. Because HDB feels like *bad news* from yesteryear. Like shit, honcho.
Psst! Her gloves -- they'll help you with the autopsy.
Psychic arts? Sounds right down our alley.
Red paint is peeling off it. Looks like blood, no?
Relax, you're not a 'new face'. She doesn't mean you. You are an outsider. And a cop, remember?
Remember this silence. The lady is dangerous. People suffer around her.
Right?! You'll have better luck next time, though. The phasmid -- finding it is your destiny.
Rising, unfolding from the reeds on a hot summer's day... like a benevolent god.
Rust peels off the bent iron posts of the swing. The wind whistles through the skeleton of the small house behind you. There's desolation... everywhere.
S-s-s-she knows perfectly well where they are! They've got one for everybody, all coded and filed away in a vast labyrinth beneath Isherwood...
SALVATION!
SOMETHING MYSTERIOUS
STOP. Don't say it!
SUGGESTION
See you tomorrow, Harry. Her voice rings in the evening air, burning...
See, see! This place is cursed!
Seems you'll never learn the answer to that question.
Serves them right! Wipe that smirk off their face with your sad, tragic small church song. Who's laughing now? No one.
Serves them right! Wipe that smirk off their face with your sad, tragic song. Who's laughing now? No one.

Shame? You haven't really been in the presence of gentlefolk either, have you? What shame is there to be felt in front of these... dockworkers, cops and hotel clerks. But to *belittle* yourself in these eyes of Eau-de-Nil...
She is perturbed by your not knowing what year it is -- but not by your mention of the apocalypse. It *must* be the end times.
She meant she sees him in her dreams.
She narrows her eyes -- turning her gaze to the future out of professional habit...
She remembers. Good.
She watches by, motionless.
She will be there. From 22.00 till 02.00.
She's already in prison here. For what happened. And she is prepared to never leave. Even after his death.
She's going to *love* it.
She's laughing at him. Belittling him.
She's over-radiated. And then some.
She's right -- those curtains give off some *eerie vibes*. Better not mess with it.
Shhhh! You shouldn't say it out loud... they'll realize you know!
Shit! The girl... the girl upstairs?! That can't be her! Remember what you said to her? You told you wanna have...
Shit! The girl... the girl upstairs?! That can't be her. She knows you drank so hard you forgot you were a cop...
Shush. He's not drinking the spirit bomb. He's putting it in his hand and he's going to be ready.
Silence is silence? You're sure there's more to it...
Silence...
Snow is quietly covering the numerous wine bottles and cigarette butts on the ground. Someone *partied* really, really hard here.
So *this* is what power feels like. Do you like it?
So close to envisioning what's beyond. Just needs a little push over the edge...
So fast as to make it seem like the weather turns at her command.
So it does. It's gone. All that's left for you -- is the disco.
So it wasn't the phasmid... a wave of disappointment washes over you.
So tell me, how does it feel -- to be close to someone, skin to skin?
So this is what it feels like to be *interviewed*...
Some sadness comes crashing over you like a bad dream.
Somehow that makes you feel... scared. You don't know why.
Somehow you managed to dodge it all... The church, the window *and* the bad book in the store. On the shelf. You made it. Across the black sea unharmed...
Something *mysterious* is going on here...
Something about it makes you feel bitter...
Something bad. Someone's night thoughts. A last resort. A bad idea.
Something in you wants to immediately *forget* about this. As if there was a reason you threw them away.
Something is happening. Stop.
Something is seriously wrong with you.
Something ominous.
Something sad and small wakes up in you... what is it trying to say?
Something tells you her life and yours are not that similar. Maybe it's because she has a boat and you have... that necktie? A pair of pants?
Something tells you her life and yours are not that similar.
Something tells you that won't be any time soon. This'll have to be one of those epic tasks that's open for a while...
Something tells you this is going to be a rough ride.
Something unpleasant twitches in your stomach.
Something's rattling within you. It feels like the first penny dropped into a piggy bank.
Somewhere below, military-grade Sad is dripping off the walls... you should say the first thing. It's honest. It will lead to introspection.
Somewhere in Mirova a beautiful woman sees the bright glow on the horizon and says to herself: "Oh my god, I shouldn't have left him..."
Soon. The time will come soon. Have patience, brave one.
Sooner or later you will stumble upon a tool mighty enough. Then we will know what's in this mysterious ice cream maker.
Steer clear of this one. There's something terrible about this one.
Still feels like there's *something* missing from that...
Still, the story influenced her choice of where to run. It must have some hidden layers...
Still, you felt it was important enough to make a mental note. That means *something*. You didn't pay attention to any of the other cigarette butts on the coast.
Stop, stop! Banish the thought. This isn't what you came for.
Stop. Just up ahead: danger.
Stop. Now. It is time.
Stop. Something is happening.
Strange things may flourish in the dark...
Strangely... like a puppet on a string. There's something off, you feel it. This was the right question. You just...
Suddenly this feels like a really, *really* bad idea.
Suddenly you feel like you don't want to hear about video rentals anymore. You don't want to hear about *any of it*. It was all shit. It's over.
Suddenly you feel sad, looking at this man and his bottle... somehow he looks *ancient*, these thoughts having chased him for years.
Suddenly you're conscious of yourself standing there, on... whatever this all is. Your arms hang down by your sides.
Suddenly you're reminded of the lieutenant's Kineema and the weird feeling about the fish and seaweed you had...
Suddenly your palms are sweaty. The church seems cold and large, somehow...
THERE ARE ANCIENT MYSTERIES DOWN THERE, COBO... ASK ME LATER.
Tell him. Tell him why it's you.
Ten seconds -- borders dissipate. Twenty seconds -- loss of self. Thirty seconds -- everything dissimilates into raw unreality.
Tequila... Tequila Sunset... Something ominous there.
Terrifying. It's a simple word. She was *bad* for humanity and you shouldn't have started thinking about her!
That gun is a joke compared to the actual greatest thing he lost.
That is true. But what comes after death?
That knowledge comes from a very dark place.
That much is true. His heart *truly* is in it. Though you wouldn't think so by looking at him.
That much is true.
That patch of reeds over there -- it's a great place to hide something. Kind of out of the way, being so close to the water.
That poor woman must have stories to tell like you wouldn't imagine...
That's a good, pragmatic approach.
That's because you know where this leads to.
That's it. The verdict.
That's not right at all. Thoughts don't exist in the brain, they float through the *air*! Your brain is but a fish swimming through them...
That's the problem. Look at what's pouring out of you...
That's what the hanged corpse called you -- Harry.
That's you alright. A black hound licking your own heels.
The 'Feld Electric' mural... you feel like you should go look at it again. Step *closer* this time.
The *blaasma gun* that shoots periwinkle blue high energy plasma! This is your chance...

The 9mm Villiers feels surprisingly light in your hand. Almost toy-like. You imagine death, sorrow and the dramatic re-imagining of Andre's face burst out of its muzzle, and it feels almost preposterous...
The Headless FALN Rider -- he could appear at any minute!
The Sun does little for the dead, and those hopelessly lost in their own minds.
The aim is to gain a deeper understanding.
The blue heart. Don't look into it.
The bore's the size of your eye. I can almost see the stars.
The camera of her mind glides over the surface of the water.
The church looks old and weather-worn. There are no lights in the windows.
The corpse is dead silent. You have no idea why you just said that.
The dice is black and filled with little silvery flakes, like snowfall.
The encroaching doom is golden red. It is inside you, but also outside, on the horizon, by the water's edge. Innermost but yet to happen -- things make sense from that angle...
The feeling continues. Warm and comfy on the cold shoreline. You hear a question rising to the surface, trying to break the sensation.
The fish-lips stay silent in response. You must be losing your mind, asking him that over and over...
The floorboards. The glass. The streets and the people. Nothing will remain.
The furnace has long been cold, despite itself. You're too sad to jerk off. This is not the first time this has happened, either.
The further out, the better.
The giant neon sign reading "HARRY DU BOIS" hanging from the Kvalsund crane can be seen all the way to Jamrock.
The implications of this are... too numerous to consider. Proceed with caution. Learn all you can before entering that dark building.
The inland sea is dark, vast, and on the surface meaningless...
The last missing pieces of a puzzle of flesh.
The last one.
The last thought in your head before waking is: maybe you shouldn't have seen that stained glass window? In the church....
The last thought in your head before waking is: you need to go to the church...
The lips of the corpse are swollen, preposterously. They are also silent. You have no idea why you just said that.
The mattress is soggy, but that linen feels more inviting than the pain and exhaustion. It's not too cold here...
The men are talking, but you swear you hear those black limbs tap on the window as the wind blows outside.
The mirage looks both sad and glorious in the mist, like an insect trapped in glass.
The name resounds like a bell in the air -- a dark gong. You get a bad feeling about it.
The past. It's a *silo* of sadness. Fermenting. You should keep away.
The pétanque players?
The return to her side.
The room feels soporific in its silence, dust particles twirling around in the air...
The same thing that happens to all humans. You will become vapour. Dissolve into frequencies. Then those frequencies will be forgotten.
The shadows play tricks on your mind...
The sickest. That is perhaps *why* it should be researched.

The stars aligned into a cosmic frown here. He has your fate decided. Bide your time, however, and let the stars continue their course -- and that frown shall turn into a smile. Only if you play along...
The sun sets for everyone.
The tape feels ominous... Upon it, the dead speak. Respect the tape!
The thought steadies your nerves. The journal stops shaking in your hand.
The throbbing in your head increases with every passing moment you gaze at this shelf...
The throttle is jammed and the brakeman's nowhere to be seen! There's no getting off this ride!
The time has come to *connect* to RVD HQ. By establishing a connection you'll have proved yourself worthy as a Remote Viewer...
The trash container. THE TRASH CONTAINER OF BAD MEMORIES.
The ululations of the limbic system have ended. All is quiet.
The view from the lookout is beautiful...
The water, the light... It's as though you're seeing it for the *first* time.
The white noise turns into a wall of mist and grey mould, bubbling, sweeping over the city... it tears up buildings and raises sidewalks into the sky. It's Revachol -- at the end of the world.
The word *it* feels strange. Such a beautiful boat deserves a proper name...
The words *daredevil driver* sound ominous to you.
The words *washed away* sound distant and strange suddenly. Somewhere far away, a dog barks.
The words feel heavy, like they're coming from the bottom of you. You've said them half a thousand times...
Their faces, blurred yet frozen as though in ambrotype. You were never *that young*, were you?
Then *you* happened. Your youth in the Thirties.
Then don't.
Then it's you. *You* will make her lungs glow. Your pain is NOT meaningless.
Then why does all of this feel like some sad, strange dream?
There *is* something mysterious about the curtains... be careful!
There has been precisely no evidence to the contrary. So yes, Yes, it's likely. You should probably go on stage and pose for a moment when you're done with this thought. See if it works.
There is also a *fourth* thing you've lost.
There is nothing. Only the reeds for him. The reeds and devastation.
There is. But you won't *like* it. Sweat forms on your brow, your hand is still on the lid...
There it is again -- *the pigs*, like Roy said. Not good at all.
There it is again! You are about to re-discover a long-lost species!
There it is! That strange *feeling* again! As if... there was more than meets the eye about that pile of roofing material.
There might be interesting details, fresh experiences, exciting rewards... you'd really ignore it all?
There seems to be. An *extremely* high pitched ring. Ultrasonic. Lena said it was very high-pitched, right? It's like something *tickles* your ear.
There was a longing in her for some sort of denouement, but it's unlikely that it was this... turning prematurely grey while being escorted from interrogation room to interrogation room...
There was a sting in your heart at the mention... before. When she said its name.
There's *much* more. Remember what it said, when it spoke.
There's a *spooky* building West of this. Take a flashlight with you, search the basement. There are secrets -- magisterial, ancient secrets that may assist you.

There's a growing sense of dread: the sound is coming from inside you but also surrounding you. It feels as though someone is standing just outside your range of vision and watching you, doing this to you...
There's a word on the tip of your tongue. Colourless, odourless. It's...
There's more to his animosity toward the Union Boss.
There's something else in there. There has to be. But -- it's like reaching your hand into muddy water, fishing around, and coming up with nothing...
There's something in the wind. Sometimes the only way to go forward is to fail first.
There's something in there. Not necessarily connected to the case -- but still...
There's something off about this woman. Tell her to show you the soles of her boots. Maybe she was at the hanging... somehow?
There's something the lieutenant isn't seeing. The universe is too irrational, too morally complex. You should convince him.
These forces of future have chosen to depict something that reminds you of... you.
They *would* do that in Graad.
They are going to hate you.
They live underground? These communists aren't men, they're *mole people*!
They lurk in the tropical pale of Semenine, casting strange magicks on everyone! Ignore the curtains -- or suffer the consequences...
Things in the night are not what they seem to be...
Things played out just as she had feared. Except *you* didn't shoot her.
This Mother of Silence sounds like a serious player. You might want to be careful, until you find out what you're dealing with.
This crane was built with a purpose which has now been fulfilled -- to move *this* container.
This feels wrong. Should you be doing this?
This feels... dangerous. Are you sure?
This has to be one of the mysteries you never got to solve...
This is *your* cloak. You can feel it.
This is a good, dangerous line of questioning. You should prod him on!
This is a man with a lot of past, but little present. And almost no future.
This is an omen, a sign from above -- don't start drinking again.
This is an omen, a sign from above -- that you need to stop drinking.
This is either an ominous or cool architectural choice -- hard to say.
This is everything I always warned you about.
This is exactly what you need!
This is just mundane garbage. What's even para-natural about this?
This is not the time to discuss preferences. It's not just your own life that will be at stake.
This is one thought you *need* to complete. Where are you?
This is the door, you already know it's the right door. This is going to be *so hard*.
This is the first time you've heard her laugh.
This is where he came out of himself. Drop by drop when he was unconscious. It took three, maybe four minutes.
This is you. Gold and orange. A sunset suite.
This is your chance to come up with a really good name for yourself. Get *creative* -- conceptualize!
This isn't funny... you have a bad feeling about this one.
This isn't gonna work without the lieutenant here. What if you *do* find a way in and it's a *trap*? Kitsuragi needs to be here, covering you.
This isn't the time for fear. Go.
This jacket reeks of loss and failure. It could be the one that the idiot in the colourful tracksuit was looking for...
This machinery is of the deeply mystical variety.
This means something to him. To know that name. Like naming a case. It's important.
This must be the *theory combat* the boiadeiro warned you about! En garde!
This must be where the *Entity* lives...
This story she will tell only before she *leaves* Martinaise. At the very end of her stay.
This will *surely* lead to a cryptozoological mystery with that *extremely rare insect*...
Those words -- "tell me something else" -- feel like something you've said innumerable times before. Your mouth is the very shape of them.
Three dangerous stripes appear onto your cheeks, telling stories of your wild soul.
Through a PA system.
Time for grim, serious, deadly, dangerous. It's not going to be fun.
Time has come to face the Source. Fear not, for the forces of the universe are supporting you in this psychic quest.
Trees likely do not understand the concept of fairness. Better watch your back from now on...
Trepidation. A tingling feeling in your stomach...
True. It feels... disrespectful.
Try to find something pretty and cool here. Then use it to win her back!
Unbelievable darkness and ruin.
Under your thrumming eyelids, you see a dizzying array of colours. You won't get off this carousel quite so easily.
Usual is boring. We don't do that.
Utterly. And it needs to be heard.
Vast, lukewarm and unknowable. Flowing in and out of sight.
Very cool. Now tune it to Sad FM. You know you want to.
Very strange... you get the sense she wouldn't have let you do this even if you did volunteer. Why?
Very, *very* unique energies indeed. Geomagnetic ley lines, one might even say.
Voices from another self? Voices from *a deeper self*?  Voices from... *early schizophrenia*?
W-w-w-wait. Haven't you heard this before?
Wait -- your old friend has prepared you for this -- at great cost to himself. Make sure you have the spirit bomb in your hand when *shit goes south*.
Wait, no! That's dangerous, she shouldn't do it!
Wait-wait, she's an *El-something*, you just know it.
Wait... What?
Was it *supposed* to?
Was that -- could it be -- the Col Do Ma Ma Daqua?! No. It's probably just your *imagination* ringing in your ear...
Was that... acceptance? It feels so *normal*. Nourishing even.
Way, *way* more.
We all told you. *Everyone* warned you.
We are *way* beyond normal already. We are in the STAR CHAMBER.
We are way out now... Way out in the West.
We must be dealing with seriously malign forces here if there are *dozens* of wards.
We'll see.
Weird... how? You're wearing the pinball maker's coat.
Well, they went through six bottles of *Potent Pilsner*, three bottles of *Commodore Red*, and almost four packs of cigarettes -- it must have been pretty hard.
Were you *supposed* to find her, even apart from the investigation, then? On M's request? No, you wouldn't do something like this. This must be a mistake.
What do you think happened to him? Do you think he was just trying to have fun -- or did he want out?
What if *you* did it?
What if *you* only appear as a large singular body, are actually a congregation of tiny organisms working in unison?
What if all of this is staged? He could have seen something that night in the yard...
What if something horrible has happened? What if they're dead. That's the bad vibe you got before.
What if the words are not directed at the people of Martinaise, or even the Coalition aerostatics above the city -- they're meant for something above even those...
What if you do -- but you just don't *remember* that you do?
What if... to truly love a boiadeiro is to float lifeless downstream?
What treasures wait in store for you?
What was that? Who's the *she*? And why do you feel so *bad' suddenly...
What's there not to want? It's got range, meaning, layers, colours, associations, the whole works!
Whatever it is, it was abandoned for *good* reason. Best to leave some stones unturned...
Whatever it is, it's messing with your concentration.
Whatever this *true life* is, you feel it's the real centrepiece of this mythology.
Whatever you may *look* like, you don't *feel* like a hired assassin.
When was the last time you kissed?
